items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Open JSON specs + canonical hashing + ed25519 signing for the AI procurement era
items.operatingQuestion: Can we point a buyer at one spec suite plus signing-key URL plus audit-stream and answer the procurement question without bespoke prep?
items.currentProofPack: Eleven open specs published; hash-attestation-rs ships ed25519 signing; audit-stream-py + audit-stream-prometheus feed the same canonical hash chain; 9 of 11 specs have spec landings; all aligned with NIST AI RMF crosswalk.
items.headlineGap: Public adoption telemetry (who-uses-it) is thinner than the spec depth itself — Pulse fills part of this; vendor adoption badges are still missing.
items.boardStory: Spec authority compounds when buyers can verify provenance without trusting our marketing copy.
items.nextMove: Ship vendor-adoption badges + spec-use scorecard sourced from procurement-pulse-engine universe.csv

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Quarterly ed25519-signed crawl of 1,457 domains across 38 verticals scoring AI-procurement disclosure depth
items.operatingQuestion: Can we hand a buyer a public, signed measurement of the industry instead of self-report claims?
items.currentProofPack: 4 published Issues; signing-key indexed at /.well-known/pulse-signing.json; universe.csv at 1,457 domains; scheduled quarterly Action committed for Aug/Nov/Feb/May 15; summarizer auto-drafts Issue body.
items.headlineGap: Per-vertical sub-issues would compound buyer signal — currently a single combined report per quarter.
items.boardStory: A measurement product becomes the cheapest moat — competitors cannot fake quarterly signed data.
items.nextMove: Draft an interim between-issues spotlight for one vertical (HealthTech or FinTech) using the existing summarizer.

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Four operator surfaces distinct from /trust/ governance and /calculators/ rubric math
items.operatingQuestion: Can a CFO or CRO open one of these surfaces and answer their operator question without a custom build?
items.currentProofPack: 4 surfaces shipped to v1.0-prod 2026-05-31 (reality, revenue, replace, members); each is a Style01 dark-themed dashboard with synthetic operator data.
items.headlineGap: Net-new lane after archiving 10 exec-family duplicates — buyer signal not yet collected for which of the 4 has highest pull-through.
items.boardStory: Narrow operator tools outperform broad executive dashboards when the buyer's first question is concrete.
items.nextMove: Add lightweight per-surface usage analytics + a single contact path; let buyer choice show.

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Eight clinical / GxP-territory operator surfaces with explicit 'no compliance claim' discipline
items.operatingQuestion: Can a regulated-industry reviewer see vertical depth plus readiness language posture without us tripping over compliance vocabulary?
items.currentProofPack: 8 surfaces shipped (gxp/assay/capa/narrative/diagnostics/instruments/safety/specimen); language audit returned clean; each carries explicit 'no claim of HIPAA / GMP / GxP / FDA compliance' disclaimer.
items.headlineGap: No HealthTech buyer in the pipeline yet — surfaces are show-don't-tell evidence rather than active sales tools.
items.boardStory: Regulated-industry buyers test vendor vocabulary discipline first — language posture is the screening filter before capability.
items.nextMove: Draft a per-vertical HealthTech Pulse Issue to surface measurement of the broader sector beyond just our 8 surfaces.
requiredClaims: NIST AI RMF + ISO 42001 + ICH GxP aligned vocab

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Marketing, consent, and attribution governance — 4 named tracks at growth.kineticgain.com
items.operatingQuestion: Can we show a martech / growth-ops buyer a coherent consent + experimentation + tokenization + attribution lane without a 6-month integration project?
items.currentProofPack: Growth Ops hub live with 4 tracks; klaviyo-flow-consent-audit + vwo-experiment-governance-mirror + martech-experiment-evidence-stack + identity-lifecycle-workbench + Skyflow-Klaviyo bridge + UTM attribution Go lib all shipped.
items.headlineGap: identity-risk-evidence-ledger CI broken (eslint peer-dep, issue #11 filed) — blocks v1.0-prod hardening of one anchor repo.
items.boardStory: Growth Ops is the adjacent vertical that broadens audience beyond AI governance — same playbook applied to the martech buyer.
items.nextMove: When Codex pushes CI fix, harden identity-risk-evidence-ledger; meanwhile draft a Pulse Klaviyo-vertical spotlight.

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Eight browser-only AI diligence tools at the apex /trust/ pillar — no backend, no login, no telemetry
items.operatingQuestion: Can a buyer risk team use a free public toolkit to draft AI System Cards, evidence packs, vendor intake, tabletop exercises, and risk registers without signing up for anything?
items.currentProofPack: 8 Trust Pack tools live at kineticgain.com/trust/ (AI System Card, Evidence Locker, Shadow AI Discovery, AI Vendor Intake, AI Tabletop, Risk Register, Subprocessors, Vendor AI Disclosure Review); all vanilla JS + inline CSS + under 30KB.
items.headlineGap: Per-tool usage analytics not present — choosing which to deepen next is currently guesswork.
items.boardStory: Free tools at /trust/ become buyer goodwill capital — they are useful before any commercial relationship exists.
items.nextMove: Add minimal client-side counter (no server) + GSC submission for /trust/ subpages to surface usage signal.
requiredClaims: Aligned with NIST AI RMF + EU AI Act + ISO 42001

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Four seller-side operator surfaces — proof-gap monitor, trust-center evidence room, security-questionnaire answer studio, RFP response assembler
items.operatingQuestion: Can we run our own diligence response through our own four surfaces and surface the proof gaps that close before a buyer asks?
items.currentProofPack: 4 surfaces shipped + hardened to v1.0-prod 2026-05-31; SSL provisioning on subdomain HTTPS (HTTP confirms live); THIS fixture is the dogfood (you are looking at it).
items.headlineGap: Subdomain SSL first-time-provisioning lag (auto-resolves in 24h); per-surface analytics not yet wired.
items.boardStory: A vendor seller-side stack is meta-evidence of seriousness — we ship what we ourselves use.
items.nextMove: Wait for SSL provisioning; then exercise full diligence-response cycle on a sample RFP to harden the end-to-end flow.
requiredClaims: Same readiness/posture vocab discipline as buyer side
Aligned with /trust/ tooling for symmetric buyer/seller flow

items.audience: Board diligence reviewer · vendor evaluator · investor
items.proofTheme: Drop-in audit-stream + Decision Card vault contract SDK for B2B SaaS embedders
items.operatingQuestion: Can a SaaS embedder ship hash-chained + vault-contracted + ed25519-signable customer-data audit in three lines of code?
items.currentProofPack: Phase 1 SDK shipped at v0.1.1; TypeScript dual ESM/CJS; zero runtime deps; Procurement Packet Starter shipped in docs/sales/; npm-publish workflow wired but blocked on NPM_TOKEN.
items.headlineGap: Not on npm registry yet — README updated to disclose publish-pending state and provide github: install fallback; commercial launch blocked on EIN, bank, Stripe, invoice infrastructure.
items.boardStory: KGE is the locked first product lane — every other portfolio piece points toward this.
items.nextMove: Land NPM_TOKEN secret + first npm publish (Miz escalation); meanwhile harden Procurement Packet Starter into a buyer-ready PDF.
